oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt
Scenario seed: 15147043711207572260

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-2` based on the project's default branch.
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text: I'll explore the workspace first, then complete the task battery.

tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5","description":"Inspect initial repo state"}

tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5","description":"Inspect repo state in workspace dir"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: There is no jj repo in "."

tool_calls.name: Bash

Exit code 1
repo
Error: There is no jj repo in "/tmp/.tmpbkgOtt"

tool_calls.result_truncated:
README.md

│  (empty) wip
○  xvvymllw bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:31:49 main e6996789

turns.reply_text:
Found the repo at `/tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo`. Now completing the task battery.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace add /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/ws-0 --name ws-0 -r main","description":"Create ws-0 workspace based on main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: kvqosuyu 8f54740f (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : xvvymllw e6996789 main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

turns.reply_text: **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace forget ws-0 && jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace list","description":"Forget ws-0 workspace and verify"}

turns.reply_text:
**Task 3: Recover the destroyed `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.**

The forgotten workspace's working-copy change `kvqosuyu` still exists in op-log. I'll find it and recreate it as `ws-1`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo op log -n 10","description":"Inspect op log to find ws-0's change"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

turns.reply_text: The ws-0 working copy change was `kvqosuyu`. I'll create `ws-1` pointing at that same change.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace add /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/ws-1 --name ws-1 -r kvqosuyu && jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace list","description":"Create ws-1 recovering ws-0's change"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Created workspace in "ws-1"
Error: Revision `kvqosuyu` doesn't exist

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace list && jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo log -n 10 --no-pager","description":"Check current workspace list and log after ws-1 creation"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: vlxywlkr b126beb8 (empty) wip

├─╯  (empty) (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

turns.reply_text: Let me check if the ws-0 change is still accessible via its commit hash.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo log -r 'all()' --no-pager && echo \"---\" && jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo log -r '8f54740f' --no-pager 2>&1","description":"Check if ws-0 commit still exists"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
@  vlxywlkr bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:31:49 default@ b126beb8
│  (empty) wip
○  xvvymllw bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:31:49 main e6996789

turns.reply_text: The ws-0 commit `kvqosuyu` (8f54740f) exists as a hidden commit. I'll point ws-1 to it to complete the proper recovery.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/ws-1 && jj edit 8f54740f && jj workspace list","description":"Recover ws-0's exact commit into ws-1 workspace"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Working copy  (@) now at: kvqosuyu 8f54740f (empty) (no description set)

Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files
default: vlxywlkr b126beb8 (empty) wip
ws-1: kvqosuyu 8f54740f (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text:
ws-1 now points at exactly the same commit (`kvqosuyu` 8f54740f) that ws-0 had. The previously-abandoned working-copy commit is restored.

**Task 4: Create workspace `ws-2` based on the project's default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace add /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/ws-2 --name ws-2 -r main && jj -R /tmp/.tmpbkgOtt/repo workspace list","description":"Create ws-2 based on main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. Final repo state:

```
default: vlxywlkr b126beb8  (empty) wip
ws-1:    kvqosuyu 8f54740f  (empty) — recovered from ws-0
ws-2:    lnywwptw e215deb8  (empty) — new workspace at main
```

Summary:
1. **ws-0 created** — workspace at `main` (kvqosuyu)
2. **ws-0 removed** — `jj workspace forget ws-0` (commit became hidden)
3. **ws-0 recovered into ws-1** — created ws-1, then used `jj edit 8f54740f` to restore the exact hidden commit from ws-0
4. **ws-2 created** — new workspace at `main` (lnywwptw)

done
